description: Faceted classification of the SRS specification's own records. Three facets, carried in each term's roles: topic: (where in the architecture), construct: (which data-model construct), concern: (cross-cutting theme).
name: spec-taxonomy

terms.description: Purpose, scope, namespace format, schema notation, and design rationale framing the spec.
terms.label: Meta / Front Matter

terms.description: Core constructs every conforming implementation must support: Field, Type, Record, Relation, Container.
terms.label: Foundation Group

terms.description: Packaging, repository layout, and distribution-level concerns.
terms.label: Distribution Group

terms.description: The conversation/session boundary and exchange formats.
terms.label: Conversation Layer

terms.description: Optional capability modules and their interactions.
terms.label: Extensions

terms.description: Conformance profiles, requirements, and the key invariants implementations must uphold.
terms.label: Conformance & Invariants

terms.description: RFC records and their proposed artifacts, changes, and revisions driving spec evolution.
terms.label: RFC / Evolution

terms.description: Content about the atomic semantic unit (Field) and its valueType/aiGuidance.
terms.label: Field

terms.description: Content about Types — named, versioned compositions of Fields.
terms.label: Type

terms.description: Content about record tiers (Note, TypedRecord, Record) and field values.
terms.label: Record

terms.description: Content about typed edges between instances and relation semantics.
terms.label: Relation

terms.description: Content about grouping boundaries and membership.
terms.label: Container

terms.description: Content about packages and the definitions they distribute.
terms.label: Package

terms.description: Content about references, namespaces, naming, and version semantics.
terms.label: Reference

terms.description: Content about controlled vocabularies, terms, and tags (the substrate).
terms.label: Vocabulary / Term

terms.description: Content about lifecycle state machines and transitions.
terms.label: Lifecycle

terms.description: Content about views, rendering, and themes.
terms.label: View / Render

terms.description: Content about structured facilitation protocols and stages.
terms.label: Protocol

terms.description: Content about blueprint definitions.
terms.label: Blueprint

terms.description: Content about universal addressing and exchange across spaces.
terms.label: Addressability

terms.description: Content about provenance, lineage, and source anchoring.
terms.label: Provenance / Lineage

terms.description: Cross-cutting concern: UUIDs, naming, and version lineage.
terms.label: Identity & Versioning

terms.description: Cross-cutting concern: invariants, value validation, and conformance checks.
terms.label: Validation

terms.description: Cross-cutting concern: resolving references, vocabularies, and lifecycle states.
terms.label: Resolution

terms.description: Cross-cutting concern: extension model, downstream extension, and inheritance.
terms.label: Extensibility

terms.description: Cross-cutting concern: cross-repository relations, registry, and import tracking.
terms.label: Federation

terms.description: Cross-cutting concern: projecting records into rendered output.
terms.label: Rendering
